mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate transmits through easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate, lifting equity prices over the next month. The signal is positive when FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, because easing supports valuation expansion and risk appetite.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of instances when FEDFUNDS is unchanged or declining, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and lower future demand, which reduces expected corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal activates when UNRATE jumps materially from its recent level.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return under trigger is not below unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales rise while housing starts remain weak, the market reads demand as consumption-led rather than construction-led, which supports near-term equity returns through stronger top-line revenue expectations. The signal requires both proxies because the composition of demand matters.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-up/HOUST-down combination does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample 5-day returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production indicates stronger current output and operating leverage, which typically supports equity returns over a quarter as firms convert higher activity into earnings revisions. The effect is strongest when INDPRO is rising from a depressed base.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if its rank correlation with forward returns is indistinguishable from zero over multiple rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in payrolls and retail sales signals stronger aggregate demand and labor income, supporting equity prices over the next month through better earnings expectations and lower recession odds. The signal is positive only when both series improve together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month and RSAFS rises vs prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-improvement state does not outperform single-series states in 20-day returns, or if the interaction coefficient flips sign in repeated rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation with a still-low policy rate implies negative real rates and continued policy support for risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is activated only when CPI is rising while FEDFUNDS remains suppressed.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL above its 6-month average and FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns do not occur more often than baseline when inflation is above trend and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or if the joint signal loses explanatory power after controlling for CPI alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in unemployment after a labor market peak reflects tightening credit risk and falling earnings confidence, which should pressure stocks lower over the next month. The signal is strongest when the unemployment rate is climbing from an already elevated level.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 8% and rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 50% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below unconditional average by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts after a prior contraction signals improving rate-sensitive demand and spillover strength in construction and durable goods, which tends to support equities over the next quarter. The effect should appear only when housing starts are accelerating from a low base.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is absent once GDP growth is controlled for in rolling out-of-sample tests.
